items.title: Lakeshore Road Estate
items.body: A focused tasting of their pinot and chardonnay lines, with a kitchen that finally does justice to the view. Hospitality was warm without being fussy—worth the drive from Kelowna for the terrace alone.

items.title: Benchland Collective
items.body: Small-lot reds with real structure; the winemaker walkthrough added context you rarely get on a weekend visit. Restaurant pacing stumbled during peak lunch, but the pairings were thoughtful.

items.title: Riverside Cellars
items.body: Value-forward bottles and an unpretentious tasting room. Less about spectacle, more about consistent quality—the kind of stop you recommend to friends who are new to the valley.
